title: Connecting boards in a multi-level hierachy
excerpt: Looking for a way to reflect the Objective/Goals/Strategy/Measures organisation strategy tool that can be used to link low level projects(measures) to organisational objectives.  I want to be able to aggregate project st…

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for a way to reflect the Objective/Goals/Strategy/Measures organisation strategy tool that can be used to link low level projects(measures) to organisational objectives. I want to be able to aggregate project status and timelines as you ascend the ladder from multiple projects supporting a sub-strat, to several sub-strats supporting a strat, to several strats supporting a goal, to several goals supporting an objective. Connect/mirror works brilliantly for two levels but not beyond. The OGSM template is almost standard now in organisations so I can’t be the first Monday user faced with this challenge. I not there are multiple apps out there that roll up data across boards. How have others solved this conundrum? Would be very interested to see how others have solved it.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about a dashboard that pulls in info from all the boards?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: TeamEasy (Easy Mondays)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @RayB

We love the Rollup Multiple Boards app from the excellent team - it’s a one-off cost and will allow you to establish a hierarchy as deep as you like. Pricing and details on their website here, you can also search for ‘Rollup Multiple Boards’ in the app marketplace through your monday account, and download it for a 10-day free trial.

Sing out if you need a hand - teameasy@easy-mondays.com

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Desiree - had a quick look and I can add the projects as separate boards but can’t work out a way to aggregate the data. Are you aware of any way to display, for example, the aggregated timeline for all projects that support a goal? This works with connect/mirror but only up two levels of connection

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @RayB ,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

It allows you to create a master board with multiple items from different low-level boards. It keeps everything in sync updates + replies, items + subitems.

171920×960 181 KB

What You Can Do -

Add the SAME ITEM ON MULTIPLE BOARDS with live sync and full context

Sync UPDATES + REPLIES in real time for items and subitems

Set up automations like “When status changes to something, mirror item to target board and group”

Create a Master Board with items/subitems from multiple boards

Add a subitem from one board as a main item on another board

Keep your boards clean and connected — no Mirror Columns or Connect Boards needed

Feel free to reach out to us at support@fortimus.co for a FREE DEMO or book a meeting here

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: What kind of columns are you looking to combine?

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking to aggregate project timelines and status as you move up the OGSM structure. For example, by the time your reach the top level (Enterprise Objective) it will provide an overview of the timeline for all projects that support that goal (via the sub-strategy, Strategy and Goals that support that Objective) and the status of all the tasks in those projects.

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the pointer Easy Monday - I’ve pinged Excellent about the licence model - is it applied to a single account or whether its enterprise wide but have had no reply - would you know at all?

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Fortimus Team - look for my email query

posts.author: RayB (Ray Burr)
posts.cookedHtml: An addendum to my original post - have developed a workable, interim solution: I have a defined hierarchy for each project, up to the enterprise objective level, with as many intermediate levels as required. As Monday only permits two levels of roll up but as many board connections as I like, so long as the Project board is directly connected to the level in question (using the defined hierarchy to guide which boards require the project information) then the information will roll up. This does make set up a bit more complex when a new project starts, but I’m building a workflow to help prompt and track the activities required.
